2604.27960.title: LLMs as ASP Programmers: Self-Correction Enables Task-Agnostic Nonmonotonic Reasoning
2604.27960.paper_summary: Recent large language models (LLMs) have achieved impressive reasoning milestones but continue to struggle with high computational costs, logical inconsistencies, and sharp performance degradation on high-complexity problems. While neuro-symbolic methods attempt to mitigate these issues by coupling LLMs with symbolic reasoners, existing approaches typically rely on monotonic logics (e.g., SMT) that cannot represent defeasible reasoning -- essential components of human cognition. We present "LLM+ASP," a framework that translates natural language into Answer Set Programming (ASP), a nonmonotonic formalism based on stable model semantics. Unlike prior "LLM+ASP" approaches that require manually authored knowledge modules, domain-specific prompts, or evaluation restricted to single problem classes, our framework operates without any per-task engineering and applies uniformly across diverse reasoning tasks. Our system utilizes an automated self-correction loop where structured feedback from the ASP solver enables iterative refinement. Evaluating across six diverse benchmarks, we demonstrate that: (1) stable model semantics allow LLMs to naturally express default rules and exceptions, outperforming SMT-based alternatives by significant margins on nonmonotonic tasks; (2) iterative self-correction is the primary driver of performance, effectively replacing the need for handcrafted domain knowledge; (3) compact in-context reference guides substantially outperform verbose documentation, revealing a "context rot" phenomenon where excessive context hinders constraint adherence.
2604.27960.paper_summary_zh: 最近的大型語言模型（LLMs）在推理方面取得了令人印象深刻的里程碑，但仍然面臨高計算成本、邏輯不一致性以及在高複雜性問題上的性能急劇下降等挑戰。雖然神經符號方法試圖通過將LLMs與符號推理器結合來減輕這些問題，但現有的方法通常依賴於單調邏輯（例如，SMT），這無法表示可推翻的推理——這是人類認知的基本組成部分。我們提出了“LLM+ASP”，一個將自然語言轉換為答案集編程（ASP）的框架，這是一種基於穩定模型語義的非單調形式主義。與之前需要手動編寫知識模塊、特定領域提示或限制在單一問題類別的評估的“LLM+ASP”方法不同，我們的框架在無需任何每個任務的工程的情況下運作，並且在各種推理任務中均可均勻應用。我們的系統利用自動自我修正循環，其中來自ASP求解器的結構化反饋使得迭代改進成為可能。在六個不同的基準測試中進行評估，我們證明了：（1）穩定模型語義允許LLMs自然表達默認規則和例外，在非單調任務中顯著超越基於SMT的替代方案；（2）迭代自我修正是性能的主要驅動力，有效取代了手工製作的領域知識的需求；（3）緊湊的上下文參考指南顯著超越冗長的文檔，揭示了“上下文衰退”現象，其中過多的上下文妨礙了約束的遵守。
2604.27960.author: Adam Ishay et.al.
